ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar Apr-04-2023 Continued To Jun-13-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Compelling Distribution Of Trust, Removing Trustee; Suspending Trustee, Appointing Petitioners As Successor Co-Trustees, And Surcharging Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-04-2023 Continued To Jun-13-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Ciaran O'Sullivan appearing via zoom for and with petitioners Rose O'Conner Abnedorth and Vincent O'Conner.
Emilie Calhoun specially appearing via zoom for respondent/trustee Mary O'Conner.

Mr. O'Sulivan stated that they attended mediation and pretty much worked it out, that unfortunately respondents counsel suddenly died in January, that since Ms. Calhoun came on board they have been working diligently, and that they anticipate filing a dismissal. Mrs. Calhoun asked the court to retain jurisdiction over the settlement agreement pursuant to CCP 664.6, and asked the trial date of May 02, 2023 be taken off calendar. The Court questions the basis for CCP 664.6. The parties will file a stipulation for the Courts consideration. The case management hearing and status of mediation are off calendar. The Petition of order compelling distribution of trust etc., is continued to June 13, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The trial date of May 2, 2023 is also continued to June 13, 2023.

Judge: Stephen Murphy. Clerk: Ateesh Datt. Deputy: Martinez. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Nov-15-2022 Continued To Jan-10-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Nov-15-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan, in court, for and with Rose O'Connor and Vincent O'Connor, Petitioners; James Green, in court, for and with Mary Ellen O'Connor, Objector.

The court reminds counsel of the costs of lengthy litigation and strongly encourages the parties to try and resolve this matter. Counsel report on their respective positions with regard to settlement of this matter and agree that they are close to an agreement but need some additional time to finalize it.

After additional discussion, the Case Management Hearing is taken off calendar. The remaining subject matters are continued to Jan-10-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ronald Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Yang. Not Reported.

Petition For Order Compelling Distribution Of Trust, Removing Trustee; Suspending Trustee, Appointing Petitioners As Successor Co-Trustees, And Surcharging Trustee
Off Calendar Nov-15-2022 Continued To Jan-10-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Compelling Distribution Of Trust, Removing Trustee; Suspending Trustee, Appointing Petitioners As Successor Co-Trustees, And Surcharging Trustee
Off Calendar Aug-30-2022 Continued To Nov-15-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan, in person, for and with Rose O'Connor Abendroth and Vincent O'Connor, Petitioners; James Green, in person, for and with Mary Ellen O'Connor, Respondent.

The court offers its tentative ruling and points to article five (5) of the trust which includes, but is not limited to, that the trust is to terminate upon the death of the Trustor, and it is the duty of the trustee to make distributions with no room for agreement. The court notes that distributions should have been made eleven (11) years ago accordingly and that it is inclined to order distribution. There is additional discussion regarding making in-kind distributions and moving to civil for a partition action. The court advises counsel that it intends to continue the Petition in this matter to Nov-15-2022 and any responses to the court's tentative ruling must be filed in writing by noon on Nov-01-2022, with courtesy copies delivered to the court by noon on Nov-04-2022. Mr. O'Sullivan points to a request to be served via email with any filings by Mr. Green. The court and counsel discuss e-service with Mr. Green agreeing to serve Mr. O'Sullivan via email moving forward. The court and counsel discuss the status of discovery. Both Mr. O'Sullivan and Mr. Green offer updates regarding discovery and Mr. O'Sullivan asks if the trial date set for Mar-14-2023 can be moved. The court advises that it will consider the request only if counsel can agree on a date. After discussion, the court agrees to move the trial date to Apr-04-2023 pursuant to counsel's request. The court urges the parties to meet and discuss to attempt to resolve this matter on their own. The court queries counsel regarding Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR). Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding ADR. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than Nov-04-2022. Mr. O'Sullivan is to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation via email by Nov-09-2022. Mr. O'Sullivan is to cc' Mr. Green in the email. Mr. Sullivan us instructed to monitor the court's register of actions for a copy of the mediation order and to serve all parties entitled to notice. The Parties are to return on Nov-15-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 for status of mediation.

The Case Management Hearing is taken off calendar. The remaining subject matter is continued to Nov-15-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Reporter: Peggy Tsujimoto. CSR#: 5229. T: 415.828.1919. E: peggytsujimoto@gmail.com.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-30-2022 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Compelling Distribution Of Trust, Removing Trustee; Suspending Trustee, Appointing Petitioners As Successor Co-Trustees, And Surcharging Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-26-2022 Continued To Aug-30-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Compelling Distribution Of Trust, Removing Trustee; Suspending Trustee, Appointing Petitioners As Successor Co-Trustees, And Surcharging Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-12-2022 Continued To Jul-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ciaran O'Sullivan, via Zoom, for and with Rose O'Connor Abendroth and Vincent O'Connor, Petitioners; James Green, in person, for and with Mary Ellen O'Connor, Respondent.

The court notes Mr. O'Sullivan's peremptory challenge and he is sworn in. Mr. O'Sullivan provides testimony in support of his position. Judge Castellanos states for the record that she does not know any of the parties and has never had any interactions with counsel or the parties they represent.

The peremptory challenge is granted, and the matter is continued to Jul-26-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Cecilia Castellanos. Examiner: Sandra Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Bastain. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Process, And For Dismissal, Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jul-06-2022 Off Calendar

Petition For Order Compelling Distribution Of Trust, Removing Trustee; Suspending Trustee, Appointing Petitioners As Successor Co-Trustees, And Surcharging Trustee
Off Calendar Jul-11-2022 Continued To Jul-12-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Process, And For Dismissal, Request For Judicial Notice
Off Calendar Jun-22-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Green for and with Mary Ellen O'Connor.
Ciarin O'Sullivan for and with Vincent O'Conner (by zoom) and for and with Rose O'Connor (by zoom).

Mr. Green asked to be sworn in and presented an oral peremptory challenge as to the petition that is set for hearing on July 11, 2022, but not as to the motion that is before the court today. Counsel presented oral argument as to whether or not service of the petition was appropriate, whether it was timely, whether or not the four different forms of service are sufficient, whether or not the peremptory challenge is timely, etc. Upon conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling. The Motion to Quash Service of Summons is denied. The peremptory challenge is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Bastain. Reporter: Judy DeAlba, CSR #5709, 650-571-1473, dealbacsr@sbcglobal.net
